  • $$ \varepsilon_{ki}(\lambda)
  • $$ \gamma_{\text{Stark}}(n_e, T)

  • $$ I_{ki}

CF-LIBS
  • Physics Reference
  • View page source

Physics Reference

Detailed equations, algorithms, and physical assumptions of the CF-LIBS forward model and iterative inversion solver.

This directory replaces previous pointers to CLAUDE.md for physics questions. CLAUDE.md is an AI-agent operating manual and is not part of the user/scientific documentation.

Documents

  • Equations — every equation CF-LIBS evaluates: Saha–Boltzmann, line emissivity, Voigt profile, Stark broadening, Boltzmann plot, closure modes, self-absorption, McWhirter criterion, Bayesian likelihood. Symbol table with units. Citations.

  • Assumptions and Validity — every assumption baked into the algorithm (LTE, optical thinness, single-zone uniformity, …) with regime of validity, failure mode, diagnostic, and options when each fails. Includes an audit checklist.

  • Inversion Algorithm — step-by-step walkthrough of the iterative CF-LIBS solver including pseudocode, rationale for common-slope fitting and iteration, code-location map, and uncertainty propagation paths.

When to Read Which

You want to …

Read

Understand what a recovered T means

Equations

Verify your spectrum is in the LTE regime

Assumptions and Validity

Decide between standard / matrix / oxide closure

Equations

Check if self-absorption is biasing your fit

Assumptions and Validity

Trace the iterative solver step-by-step

Inversion Algorithm

Cite the underlying physics in a paper

Equations
